title: Vulnerability SLA Compliance
dataSources: Scan results with detection timestamps, SLA policy lookup
spl:
index=vulnerability sourcetype="tenable:vuln" state="Active"
| eval age_days=round((now()-first_seen)/86400)
| eval sla_days=case(severity="Critical",7, severity="High",30, severity="Medium",90, 1=1,180)
| eval sla_status=if(age_days>sla_days,"Overdue","Compliant")
| stats count by severity, sla_status
description: SLA tracking ensures vulnerabilities are remediated within policy timeframes. Non-compliance creates audit findings.
value: SLA tracking ensures vulnerabilities are remediated within policy timeframes. Non-compliance creates audit findings.
implementation: Define SLA targets per severity. Calculate vulnerability age against SLA. Track compliance percentage. Alert when critical/high vulns approach SLA deadline. Produce compliance reports for audit evidence.
visualization: Gauge (SLA compliance %), Table (overdue vulnerabilities), Bar chart (compliance by severity), Line chart (compliance trend).
knownFalsePositives: Vulnerabilities with age above SLA on assets with documented exception, vendor patches not yet released, or planned remediation in the next window. Exception expirations from approved business decisions, vendor delays, or migrations not yet completed.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Vuln management TA.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Scan results with detection timestamps, SLA policy lookup.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Define SLA targets per severity. Calculate vulnerability age against SLA. Track compliance percentage. Alert when critical/high vulns approach SLA deadline. Produce compliance reports for audit evidence.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vulnerability sourcetype="tenable:vuln" state="Active"
| eval age_days=round((now()-first_seen)/86400)
| eval sla_days=case(severity="Critical",7, severity="High",30, severity="Medium",90, 1=1,180)
| eval sla_status=if(age_days>sla_days,"Overdue","Compliant")
| stats count by severity, sla_status
```

Understanding this SPL

**Vulnerability SLA Compliance** — SLA tracking ensures vulnerabilities are remediated within policy timeframes. Non-compliance creates audit findings.

Documented **Data sources**: Scan results with detection timestamps, SLA policy lookup. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Vuln management TA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vulnerability; **sourcetype**: tenable:vuln. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vulnerability, sourcetype="tenable:vuln". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sla_days** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sla_status** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by severity, sla_status** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare age and overdue counts in Splunk with Tenable.io or Qualys VMDR dashboards and with your published SLA and exception register. A finding can be "overdue" in math but still in policy if an approved exception exists.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gauge (SLA compliance %), Table (overdue vulnerabilities), Bar chart (compliance by severity), Line chart (compliance trend).
grandmaExplanation: We check which open issues have sat past the fix times we promised so audits and service owners see gaps before they become incidents.
